entries.main_competence: 1.0 Manage information search from different sources for lifelong learning
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use ICT tools to search for information from different sources
entries.learning_activity: (a) Familiarise with various types of search engines (e.g., Google, Google Scholar, Bing)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming different types of search engines and the information they can search through them
Practical: Guide the students in groups through completing web search tasks to find information on a given topic
entries.assessment: Various types of search engines are familiarised effectively
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming different types of search engines and the information they can search through them
Practical: Guide the students in groups through completing web search tasks to find information on a given topic
entries.assessment: Various types of search engines are familiarised effectively
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Manage information search from different sources for lifelong learning
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use ICT tools to search for information from different sources
entries.learning_activity: (b) Utilise ICT tools to search for general information (e.g., books, journals)
teaching_learning_activities: Practical: Guide the students through completing web search tasks to find books, journals, articles, and images and storing them for use
entries.assessment: ICT tools are utilised effectively in searching for general information
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
entries.learning_activity: (a) Answer questions based on information from presentations/tasks
teaching_learning_activities: Presentation: Make a presentation on a selected topic to the students and ask them questions
Guide the students in groups through presenting topics and responding to questions from others’ presentations
entries.assessment: Questions based on information from presentations/tasks are answered correctly
resources: Texts about a wide range of events
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.assessment: Questions based on information from presentations/tasks are answered correctly

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
entries.learning_activity: (b) Practise pronunciation of words from oral and audio/audio-visual sources
teaching_learning_activities: Tongue twisters: Play recorded tongue twisters for students to listen and note down new words they learned to pronounce
Reciting: Guide the students through reciting the tongue twisters by pronouncing words correctly
Songs: Play recorded authentic songs with the appropriate pronunciations for students to listen. Then, guide the students through singing the songs by pronouncing words correctly
entries.assessment: Pronunciation of words from oral and audio/audio-visual sources is practised appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
entries.learning_activity: (b) Practise pronunciation of words from oral and audio/audio-visual sources
teaching_learning_activities: Tongue twisters: Play recorded tongue twisters for students to listen and note down new words they learned to pronounce
Reciting: Guide the students through reciting the tongue twisters by pronouncing words correctly
Songs: Play recorded authentic songs with the appropriate pronunciations for students to listen. Then, guide the students through singing the songs by pronouncing words correctly
entries.assessment: Pronunciation of words from oral and audio/audio-visual sources is practised appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
entries.learning_activity: (c) Write words heard from oral and audio/audio-visual sources
teaching_learning_activities: Dictation: Guide the students through writing the words heard from audio/audio-visual recordings
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through writing sentences using the words heard
entries.assessment: Words heard from oral and audio/audio-visual sources are written
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
entries.learning_activity: (d) Reproduce messages from oral presentations
teaching_learning_activities: Imitation: Guide the students through reproducing messages from the information presented orally
Whispering game/Message passing : Guide the students through listening to oral information and passing it on to others
entries.assessment: Messages from oral presentations are reproduced accurately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (a) Distinguish words with minimal sound distinctions that have different meanings (cut/cat; paper/pepper; date/debt)
teaching_learning_activities: Word cards: Use an appropriate number of cards to write minimal pairs and ask the students to distinguish their sounds and meanings
Tongue twisters : Guide the students through pronouncing tongue twisters with minimal pairs

Minimal pairs games: Guide the students through pronouncing words with nearly similar sounds by using games (e.g., Is it a pair ?, odd one out , minimal pair up, minimal pair slap , minimal pairs pelmanism, and pronunciation journey)
entries.assessment: Words with minimal sound distinctions that have different meanings are distinguished correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (a) Distinguish words with minimal sound distinctions that have different meanings (cut/cat; paper/pepper; date/debt)
teaching_learning_activities: Word cards: Use an appropriate number of cards to write minimal pairs and ask the students to distinguish their sounds and meanings
Tongue twisters : Guide the students through pronouncing tongue twisters with minimal pairs

Minimal pairs games: Guide the students through pronouncing words with nearly similar sounds by using games (e.g., Is it a pair ?, odd one out , minimal pair up, minimal pair slap , minimal pairs pelmanism, and pronunciation journey)
entries.assessment: Words with minimal sound distinctions that have different meanings are distinguished correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (b) Practise pronunciation with proper word stress assignment
teaching_learning_activities: Mimic : Use ICT to play word pronunciation with appropriate stress placement and ask the students to mimic
Dictionary use : Use a dictionary to show the students how to indicate stress on syllables of words, and guide them through pronouncing the words
Games: Use games like Clap or Clicking fingers on the stresse d syllables and mumbling stress pattern
entries.assessment: Pronunciation with proper word stress assignment is practised appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (b) Practise pronunciation with proper word stress assignment
teaching_learning_activities: Mimic : Use ICT to play word pronunciation with appropriate stress placement and ask the students to mimic
Dictionary use : Use a dictionary to show the students how to indicate stress on syllables of words, and guide them through pronouncing the words
Games: Use games like Clap or Clicking fingers on the stresse d syllables and mumbling stress pattern
entries.assessment: Pronunciation with proper word stress assignment is practised appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (c) Practise pronunciation of words with short and long vowels (e.g., sit/seat, hit/heat, pot/port)
teaching_learning_activities: Mimic: Use ICT to play pronunciation of words with short and long vowels and ask the students to mimic
Games: Use games like ‘Same or Different’ and ‘Pronunciation Maze’ to guide the students through pronouncing words with short and long vowels
entries.assessment: Pronunciation of words with short and long vowels is practised appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (d) Practise pronunciation of unfamiliar sounds (e.g., through the use of tongue twisters, songs, poems)
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through searching for unfamiliar words from different sources and presenting their work to the class
Tongue twisters: Guide the students through pronouncing tongue twisters with unfamiliar sounds
Songs: Guide the students through singing a song with unfamiliar sounds
Poems: Guide the students through reading aloud and reciting poems with unfamiliar sounds
Mimic: Use ICT to play the pronunciation of unfamiliar sounds and ask the students to mimic
entries.assessment: Pronunciation of unfamiliar sounds is practised appropriately through the use of various techniques
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (e) Practise pronunciation of words with similar sounds that have different spellings (e.g., one/won, ewe/you)
teaching_learning_activities: Drill: Guide the students through choral and individual repetition to pronounce correctly words with similar sounds
Games: Guide the students through pronouncing words with similar sounds but different spellings by using games (e.g., Is it a pair?, odd one out, pelmanism and homophone go fish)
entries.assessment: Pronunciation of words with similar sounds but different spellings is practised appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
entries.learning_activity: (e) Practise pronunciation of words with similar sounds that have different spellings (e.g., one/won, ewe/you)
teaching_learning_activities: Drill: Guide the students through choral and individual repetition to pronounce correctly words with similar sounds
Games: Guide the students through pronouncing words with similar sounds but different spellings by using games (e.g., Is it a pair?, odd one out, pelmanism and homophone go fish)
entries.assessment: Pronunciation of words with similar sounds but different spellings is practised appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Write new vocabulary from conversations and written texts on particular topics (e.g., politics, sports, tourism, agriculture)
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing a particular topic and writing the vocabulary heard and their meanings
Dialogue: Guide the students through participating in a dialogue and writing new vocabulary from it
Extensive reading: Guide the students through reading texts to acquire vocabulary for specific fields and writing new vocabulary
entries.assessment: New vocabulary from conversations and written texts on particular topics are written correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Write new vocabulary from conversations and written texts on particular topics (e.g., politics, sports, tourism, agriculture)
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing a particular topic and writing the vocabulary heard and their meanings
Dialogue: Guide the students through participating in a dialogue and writing new vocabulary from it
Extensive reading: Guide the students through reading texts to acquire vocabulary for specific fields and writing new vocabulary
entries.assessment: New vocabulary from conversations and written texts on particular topics are written correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Construct sentences using vocabulary acquired from conversations and written texts
teaching_learning_activities: Dialogue: Guide the students through acting out a dialogue and constructing sentences using the new vocabulary learned
Debate: Guide the students through participating in a debate and constructing sentences using the new vocabulary learned
Spotting words : Guide the students through identifying new vocabulary from written texts and constructing sentences using the words
Close reading: Guide the students through reading texts and constructing sentences using the new words learned
entries.assessment: Sentences are constructed correctly using vocabulary acquired from conversations and written texts
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Play vocabulary building games using digital and non-digital resources (e.g., word board games, charades, picture games, flash cards)
teaching_learning_activities: Games: Guide the students through playing vocabulary building games
Questions and answers: Ask the students some questions about the new vocabulary they have learned
Collaborative practice : Guide the students in pairs/groups through constructing sentences using the new vocabulary they have learned
entries.assessment: V ocabulary building games are played accurately using digital and non-digital resources
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (a) Express ideas or opinions in contextualised oral and written language tasks using proper grammar and vocabulary (e.g hospital, market, library, bus stop, sport and games)
teaching_learning_activities: Conversations : Guide the students through expressing their opinions orally and in writing by participating in conversations (e.g., discussion, debate, dialogue, bargaining) on controversial issues
Scenario: Present some controversial cases and ask the students to discuss and express their opinions orally and in writing.
Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplaying radio/TV programmes by giving opinions on given topics
entries.assessment: Ideas or opinions are expressed correctly in contextualised oral and written language tasks using proper grammar and vocabulary
resources: Audio and audio-visual materials (audio clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (a) Express ideas or opinions in contextualised oral and written language tasks using proper grammar and vocabulary (e.g hospital, market, library, bus stop, sport and games)
teaching_learning_activities: Conversations : Guide the students through expressing their opinions orally and in writing by participating in conversations (e.g., discussion, debate, dialogue, bargaining) on controversial issues
Scenario: Present some controversial cases and ask the students to discuss and express their opinions orally and in writing.
Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplaying radio/TV programmes by giving opinions on given topics
entries.assessment: Ideas or opinions are expressed correctly in contextualised oral and written language tasks using proper grammar and vocabulary
resources: Audio and audio-visual materials (audio clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (b) Construct sentences using appropriate grammar and vocabulary relating to a specific context (structure: tenses – simple past, simple present, articles, present progressive, pronouns, coordinators, prepositions, adjectives, adverbs)
teaching_learning_activities: Description: Guide the students through describing objects, events, and places using appropriate grammar and vocabulary
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through writing sentences in different tenses and discussing why the sentences are written in those tenses
Group work : Guide the students in groups through constructing sentences orally and in writing which describe qualities using pronouns, coordinators, prepositions, adjectives and adverbs
entries.assessment: Sentences are constructed correctly using appropriate grammar and vocabulary relating to a specific context
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks

teaching_learning_activities: Description: Guide the students through describing objects, events, and places using appropriate grammar and vocabulary
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through writing sentences in different tenses and discussing why the sentences are written in those tenses
Group work : Guide the students in groups through constructing sentences orally and in writing which describe qualities using pronouns, coordinators, prepositions, adjectives and adverbs
entries.assessment: Sentences are constructed correctly using appropriate grammar and vocabulary relating to a specific context
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (c) Debate on topics using proper grammar and vocabulary
teaching_learning_activities: Debates: Guide the students through conducting debates on cross-cutting topics using proper grammar and vocabulary
entries.assessment: Topics are debated appropriately using proper grammar and vocabulary
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension
entries.learning_activity: (a) Sequence the order of events in stories
teaching_learning_activities: Reading stories : Guide the students through reading stories with sequence words. (e.g., next, then, after, earlier, first, second, and later) Then, ask them to identify the words and use them to write a paragraph
Filling the missing parts: Give the students stories with skipped parts and ask them to fill in the missing parts using sequence words
Strip story: Guide the students through sequencing the order of events in stories using strip story. Then, let them read their stories aloud in the classroom
Story completion: Provide the students with a beginning part of a story and guide them through completing it using sequence words
Jumbled texts : Use jumbled texts to guide the students through ordering the sequence of events in the stories
entries.assessment: The order of events in a story is sequenced logically
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension

Story completion: Provide the students with a beginning part of a story and guide them through completing it using sequence words
Jumbled texts : Use jumbled texts to guide the students through ordering the sequence of events in the stories
entries.assessment: The order of events in a story is sequenced logically
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension
entries.learning_activity: (b) Make a summary of stories
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing the main issues in a story
5Ws and 1H, First Then Finally, SWBST, and SAAC Method: Guide the students through summarising various stories using the aforementioned methods
entries.assessment: Stories are summarised correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension
entries.learning_activity: (c) Retell stories
teaching_learning_activities: Retelling stories: Guide the students in pairs through retelling stories by taking turns
Narration: Guide the students through narrating to others about story books and appropriate movies they have watched
entries.assessment: Stories are retold accordingly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
entries.learning_activity: (a) Paraphrase various oral messages from different contexts
teaching_learning_activities: Paraphrasing: Read passages orally, play recorded texts and guide the students through paraphrasing the messages
Project: Ask the students to listen to various oral messages (presentations, on radio, TV) and paraphrase the messages for presentation in the classroom
entries.assessment: Various oral messages are paraphrased appropriately from different contexts
resources: audio and audio-visual materials (audio clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
entries.learning_activity: (b) Respond to oral messages from different contexts
teaching_learning_activities: Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplay to respond to oral instructions, information and orders
Telephone conversation : Guide the students through responding to interrogations via telephone conversations
entries.assessment: Oral messages from different contexts are responded to correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
entries.learning_activity: (c) Respond to questions about oral messages
teaching_learning_activities: Panel interviews: Guide the students through responding to questions in panel interviews
entries.assessment: Questions about oral messages are responded to correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Express ideas and opinions in different interpersonal communication settings (e.g., I think…, I guess…, In my opinion…, I would say …)
teaching_learning_activities: Information search : Guide the students through seeking information on expressing ideas and opinions in interpersonal communication
Scenario: Use a controversial scenario to guide the students through expressing their opinions orally and in writing
entries.assessment: Ideas and opinions are expressed correctly in different interpersonal communication settings
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Use appropriate non-verbal cues in interpersonal communication
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing the meaning of different non-verbal cues and presenting the work to the class
Act it out: Guide the students in groups through acting and interpreting non-verbal cues
entries.assessment: Appropriate non-verbal cues are used correctly in interpersonal communication
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Use proper grammar and vocabulary in different interpersonal communication contexts (e.g., greetings, biding farewell, apologising)
teaching_learning_activities: Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplaying greeting guests, bidding farewell and apologising using proper grammar and vocabulary
entries.assessment: Grammar and vocabulary are used correctly in different interpersonal communication contexts
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Comprehend oral and written information
entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Use appropriate language and expressions in communicating with people of different age groups and statuses (e.g., peers, elders, family members, leaders)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students in groups through brainstorming ideas on the differences in conversations between different groups of people
Dialogue: Guide the students through participating in dialogue among different age groups and statuses
entries.assessment: Appropriate language and expressions are used with different age groups and statuses
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Express daily routines (structure: present simple)
teaching_learning_activities: Real-life expression: Guide the students through expressing orally what they do usually
Inquiry: Guide the students through inquiring what famous people in their community do usually and presenting their work before the class
Interview: Guide the students in pairs through interviewing each other about their fact routines
entries.assessment: Daily routines are expressed correctly
resources: maps of various places (e.g., school, village)
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Express ongoing activities (structure: present progressive)
teaching_learning_activities: Description: Guide the students through describing events as they occur (e.g., wedding ceremonies, birthday parties, burial ceremonies, processes, and public events) using the present progressive tense
entries.assessment: Ongoing activities are expressed correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Express family relationship and occupations (structure: present simple+progressive)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students in pairs through brainstorming ideas about family relationship and different kinds of occupations
Songs and games : Guide the students through singing songs and playing games about family relationships and occupations
Matching: Guide the students through matching pictures with occupations and occupations with their descriptions
The use of pictures: Display pictures of people with different occupations and ask the students to express their occupations using the present simple+progressive tenses
Real-life expression: Ask the students to express the occupations of their parents/guardians and other relatives using the present simple+progressive tenses
entries.assessment: Family relationship and occupations are expressed correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Express ownership/possession (structure: have/has in present simple and had in past simple, apostrophe, possessive pronouns)
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through reading texts on expressing possession using have, has, had, apostrophe, and possessive pronouns
Real-life expression: Guide the students through expressing possessions using the real objects they posses
Conversation: Guide the students in groups through engaging in conversations about what their relatives and neighbours posses
entries.assessment: Ownership/possession is expressed correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (e) Give directions
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through searching for information on how to ask for and give directions and present the work to the class
Map reading: Guide the students in pairs through directing each other how to reach various stations/points (from one point to another) as indicated in the map
Group work: Guide the students in groups through asking and giving directions on how to reach various places (e.g., offices, classrooms, and toilets) within the school compound
entries.assessment: Directions are given appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (e) Give directions
teaching_learning_activities: Information search: Guide the students through searching for information on how to ask for and give directions and present the work to the class
Map reading: Guide the students in pairs through directing each other how to reach various stations/points (from one point to another) as indicated in the map
Group work: Guide the students in groups through asking and giving directions on how to reach various places (e.g., offices, classrooms, and toilets) within the school compound
entries.assessment: Directions are given appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (a) Recognise the main stages of writing texts ( e.g., pre-writing, drafting, revising, editing)
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing stages of writing texts and presenting their work to the class
Demonstration: Demonstrate how to begin writing by asking the students to give ideas and help you plan the text
entries.assessment: The stages of writing texts are recognised appropriately
resources: samples of friendly letters and invitations
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (a) Recognise the main stages of writing texts ( e.g., pre-writing, drafting, revising, editing)
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing stages of writing texts and presenting their work to the class
Demonstration: Demonstrate how to begin writing by asking the students to give ideas and help you plan the text
entries.assessment: The stages of writing texts are recognised appropriately
resources: samples of friendly letters and invitations
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (b) Write a variety of friendly letters and invitations (vocabulary: Dear Father/Uncle, Dear Mother/Aunt, Mr/Mrs…, Dr./Prof./Madam/Ms …)
teaching_learning_activities: Observation: Display/distribute samples of friendly letters and invitations for the students to study and discover their parts
Collaboration : Guide the students through collaborating in writing friendly letters and invitations
entries.assessment: A variety of friendly letters and invitations are written correctly with appropriate vocabulary
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (b) Write a variety of friendly letters and invitations (vocabulary: Dear Father/Uncle, Dear Mother/Aunt, Mr/Mrs…, Dr./Prof./Madam/Ms …)
teaching_learning_activities: Observation: Display/distribute samples of friendly letters and invitations for the students to study and discover their parts
Collaboration : Guide the students through collaborating in writing friendly letters and invitations
entries.assessment: A variety of friendly letters and invitations are written correctly with appropriate vocabulary
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (c) Create dialogues on various topics (e.g., the value of education, environment, culture, gender equality)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students in groups through brainstorming ideas on the meaning and structure of dialogues
Observation: Guide the students through studying a prepared dialogue and writing down the key features they observe
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through creating dialogues on various topics
Act it out: Guide the students in pairs through acti ng out the dialogues they have prepared
entries.assessment: Dialogues on various topics are created appropriately
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Communicate effectively in different contexts
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (d) Create and express orally daily and weekly timetables for routines
teaching_learning_activities: Real-life expression: Guide the students through expressing orally and in writing what they do weekly
Inquiry: Guide the students through inquiring what famous people do weekly and reporting to the classroom
Interview: Guide the students through interviewing each other in pairs about their fact routines and timetables
Planning and preparing : Guide the students individually through planning and preparing their w eekly timetables and presenting them to the class
entries.assessment: Daily and weekly time-tables for routines are created and expressed correctly
entries.references: TIE, English Language for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
